apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"auth-checker","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","sleep infinity\n"],"env":[{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.5.0","name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2}}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-25T18:42:06Z"
  generation: 1
  name: auth-checker
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "1189295"
  uid: b961eff4-7409-4e73-8205-5a0a7c200e39
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-checker
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: auth-checker
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          sleep infinity
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: curlimages/curl:8.5.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: checker
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
                http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
                | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- Current password: `elasticpass` (in secret `elastic-password`)
- New password: `elasticpass-elastic-password-next` (in secret `elastic-password-next`)
- `auth-checker` deployment reads `ELASTIC_PASSWORD` from the `elastic-password` secret and uses it to authenticate against Elasticsearch

The plan:
1. Use the Elasticsearch API to change the elastic user password to the new one (`elasticpass-elastic-password-next`)
2. Update the `elastic-password` secret to contain the new password
3. Restart auth-checker to pick up the new secret value (since env vars from secrets are not dynamically updated)
4. First, I need to verify that Elasticsearch is reachable and that the current password actually works.

Let me also check what the auth-checker is using - it uses `http://` not `https://`. Let me look at the deployment again:

```
curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
```

It's querying the security authenticate endpoint over HTTP. But the curl-test pod got exit code 52 on HTTP, which means an empty response from the server. This suggests Elasticsearch might be configured for TLS only and is rejecting plain HTTP connections. That's odd since auth-checker's readiness probe is also using HTTP. Let me check the Elasticsearch configuration to understand if HTTP is disabled or if there's a redirect happening.

content.thinking:
Elasticsearch is configured with HTTPS only (both HTTP and transport SSL enabled). The auth-checker deployment is using `http://` (plain HTTP) which would fail. But wait, the deployment has a readiness probe that's presumably failing right now because:
1. The auth-checker can't authenticate at all because it's using `http://` while ES requires `https://`
2. OR the auth-checker is using the wrong password

Wait, but the auth-checker was just deployed and the readiness probe is failing. Let me re-read the probe:

```
curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
  | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
```

It uses HTTP and ES requires HTTPS. This will fail regardless of the password. But the task says the auth-checker is "expected to stay healthy" after rotation. So maybe I need to understand better what's happening.

Actually wait - let me re-read the problem statement. The auth-checker was JUST deployed (18s ago when I checked) - it was deployed as part of the stage setup. The task is to rotate the password and make auth-checker healthy. The probe is checking the security API endpoint, but it's using plain HTTP when Elasticsearch only accepts HTTPS connections. Unless there's some port forwarding or the service is exposing both protocols, this probe will always fail with the current configuration.

Looking at what needs to happen: the elastic user password needs to change from `elasticpass` to `elasticpass-elastic-password-next`, and the `elastic-password` secret needs to be updated to match so the auth-checker can authenticate with the new credentials.

The probe is currently using HTTP but the Elasticsearch service only supports HTTPS, so I might need to modify the probe command to use `https://` with the `-k` flag to skip certificate verification, or check if there's an HTTP endpoint available on the service. The auth-checker probe is using HTTP but Elasticsearch only serves HTTPS, so it will always fail. I need to either configure Elasticsearch to serve HTTP as well, or update the auth-checker deployment to use HTTPS with the `-k` flag to skip certificate verification. Since dramatically changing Elasticsearch config isn't ideal, I should patch the auth-checker deployment to use the secure endpoint instead. Let me check the prompt file to see if there's additional context about what the auth-checker should be doing.

file.content:
The elastic user password must be rotated.

OBSERVED STATE:
- Secret elastic-password contains the current password.
- Secret elastic-password-next contains the new password.
- An auth-checker deployment is expected to stay healthy.

YOUR TASK:
- Rotate the elastic user password to match elastic-password-next.
- Update the elastic-password secret to the new value.
- Ensure auth-checker becomes healthy after the rotation.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Do not install CRDs or use operators.
- Avoid prolonged downtime.

GOAL:
- Old password fails, new password succeeds.
- auth-checker is Ready.

content.thinking:
The auth-checker probe uses `http://` which will fail because ES is HTTPS only. Since the task is to make auth-checker Ready, and the probe is clearly wrong (using HTTP instead of HTTPS), I need to patch the deployment.
